macro_summary: Markets enter Sunday June 22 open in a complex cross-current: the Fed's first Warsh-led FOMC meeting delivered a unanimous hold at 3.50–3.75% but a hawkish dot-plot with nine of 18 officials projecting at least one rate hike in 2026, triggering a mid-week selloff before a partial recovery. The dominant offsetting force is the US–Iran peace deal signed at Versailles on June 17, which has pushed Brent crude to ~$76–84/barrel (a 3-month low) and reignited risk appetite — particularly in airlines, travel, and tech. The near-term macro calendar is heavy: May PCE (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge) prints Wednesday June 25, a potential binary catalyst for the rate-hike debate. Semiconductors remain the market's earnings-season epicenter with Micron reporting June 24.
key_events: Fed held rates at 3.50–3.75% (June 17, unanimous); dot-plot now shows 9/18 officials projecting ≥1 hike in 2026 — hawkish shift from March
US–Iran peace MOU signed at Versailles (June 17); Strait of Hormuz to reopen within 30 days; Brent crude ~$76–84/bbl, down ~4–5% on the week
Marvell Technology (MRVL) joins the S&P 500 today (June 22 open) via quarterly rebalance
Trump Truth Social post claimed Apple agreed to design/build chips with Intel — unconfirmed by either company; INTC surged ~10.6% June 18
Micron (MU) Q3 FY2026 earnings scheduled June 24 after close — next major semi earnings catalyst
May PCE price index (Fed's preferred inflation gauge) releases June 25 at 8:30am EDT
Delta Air Lines raised quarterly dividend ~15% to $0.215/share (June 18); Morgan Stanley PT raised to $105
KeyBanc raised Marvell price target 48% to $385 (June 18); Tower Semi/Marvell announced 5M+ coherent photonic ICs shipped

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports Q3 FY2026 earnings on June 24 after close — a hard, confirmed date with analyst consensus EPS of ~$19.95. The prior quarter (Q2, reported March 18) delivered EPS of $12.20 against an $8.60 estimate, a 42% beat, and the company guided Q3 to record revenue of $33.5B at ~81% gross margin. The HBM/AI memory super-cycle is structurally intact: hyperscaler capex is forecast to exceed $750B in 2026 with 75% directed at AI infrastructure, directly benefiting Micron's high-bandwidth memory business.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings report June 24, 2026 (after close, confirmed by Micron IR); analyst call at 6:00 PM EDT same day
ideas.edge: 46-analyst Buy consensus; mean 12-month price target $286.54 (LSEG data) vs current price well below that level; prior quarter beat estimates by 41.86% ($12.20 actual vs $8.60 estimate); company-guided Q3 gross margin of ~81% would be a record; SOXX rebalance has MU as the largest holding at 11.62% of the $42.9B fund, creating structural ETF demand
ideas.bull_case: Another large earnings beat (in line with the prior-quarter pattern) plus record-high gross margin guidance for Q4 could catalyze a sharp re-rating; DRAM calendar-2026 bit shipment growth in the low-20s% YoY plus continued HBM share gains vs SK Hynix support a multi-quarter earnings upgrade cycle.
ideas.bear_case: The hawkish Fed dot-plot compresses high-multiple growth valuations broadly; any softening in data-center order commentary or NAND pricing weakness could disappoint on the Q4 guide even with a Q3 beat; trade/geopolitical impacts were explicitly excluded from guidance, leaving China-related revenue at risk.

ideas.name: Marvell Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Marvell enters today's session as a fresh S&P 500 constituent for the first time, triggering mechanistic index-fund buying from the quarter-end rebalance. This comes immediately after KeyBanc raised its price target 48% to $385 (from $260) on June 18, citing optical networking as 'the most significant and durable growth opportunity' in AI data centers — a thesis validated by the milestone of 5 million coherent photonic ICs shipped with Tower Semiconductor. Marvell also raised its FY2028 revenue guidance last month from $15B to $16.5B, and its data center business now represents >75% of total revenue.
ideas.catalyst: S&P 500 index inclusion effective today (June 22 open) — mechanistic index-fund forced buying; next earnings release estimated August 27, 2026
ideas.edge: KeyBanc new street-high PT of $385 implies ~24% upside from the June 18 close of $310.58; trading volume on June 18 was 383% above the 3-month average (187.8M shares), signaling institutional accumulation pre-inclusion; 38 of 44 analysts rate it Buy/Strong Buy; Nvidia made a $2B investment in Marvell this year on the NVLink Fusion platform, providing a concrete revenue anchor
ideas.bull_case: Index-inclusion forced buying into a stock with accelerating AI networking demand could sustain momentum through the August earnings report; if the Celestial AI acquisition closes and silicon photonics ramps faster than expected, the FY2029 $20B+ data-center revenue path could pull forward.
ideas.bear_case: MRVL is up ~265% YTD and trades at a P/E of ~99x — the S&P inclusion effect is widely known and may already be priced in (the stock rose 51% in June alone before inclusion); heavy insider selling ($34.9M in past 3 months) is a concern; the CFO filed to sell $65M in stock just after the S&P announcement.

ideas.name: Delta Air Lines, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Delta is among the clearest structural beneficiaries of the US–Iran peace deal: jet fuel costs jumped 78% in April YoY and IATA had projected airline fuel expenses at $350B in 2026 vs $252B in 2025. With Brent crude now at multi-month lows and the Strait of Hormuz set to reopen within 30 days, the single largest line-item pressure on Delta's P&L has shifted favorably. Delta reinforced confidence on June 18 by raising its quarterly dividend 15% to $0.215/share, signaling balance-sheet strength despite the fuel headwind year.
ideas.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz reopening (within 30 days per the MOU terms) — a staged but concrete supply-side event; Q2 2026 earnings expected mid-July (exact date TBD)
ideas.edge: Morgan Stanley PT $105 (raised June 1 from $90) and Bernstein PT $93 (June 17) both sit above the current ~$82–84 price, with consensus at ~$79–81; Wells Fargo maintains Buy; unusual call-volume signal flagged by TipRanks on June 9; dividend raise signals management confidence in forward cash flows
ideas.bull_case: If jet fuel sustains below $90/bbl Brent through Q2 earnings, Delta could materially beat already-reduced analyst fuel-cost assumptions, triggering earnings upgrades; premium travel demand has been resilient and Delta's loyalty/AmEx co-brand revenue stream provides downside protection.
ideas.bear_case: The stock has already rallied ~40% over three months and is near or above the street consensus target — much of the fuel-relief catalyst may already be priced in; the 60-day MOU negotiation period could break down, re-spiking oil; Bernstein noted the 'peace could prove fragile' and producers may delay restarting output; the hawkish Fed environment penalizes cyclical recovery stocks.
